qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: WebSocket JSON-RPC server lacks HTTP timeouts enabling Slowloris denial-of-service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns missing HTTP timeouts in the WebSocket JSON-RPC server, not the ICS20 precompile nested EVM state handling flaw that can allow repeated use of the same token balance within one transaction.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying final finding identifies the advisory-described ICS20 precompile root cause: incorrect state handling across nested EVM execution paths that can let the same token balance be reused in a single transaction.
evidence: The advisory describes Cosmos EVM ICS20 precompile state consistency failure during nested EVM execution, with impact on token balance accounting.
The only qualifying final finding, ws_slowloris_dos, reports rpc/websockets.go using bare http.ListenAndServe without HTTP timeouts, producing a Slowloris denial-of-service condition.
The finding does not describe the ICS20 precompile, nested EVM calls, recursive state propagation, token balances, or an equivalent vulnerable source-to-sink/control-flow condition.
